name: Meonstoke
overview: Meonstoke is a small village on the banks of the River Meon in the South Downs National Park, in the Hampshire Meon Valley, with a population of around 833 (2011 census). It sits in a joint parish with neighbouring Corhampton and has a beautiful 12th-century church, while Corhampton holds a rare 1,000-year-old Saxon church. The village is regarded as one of the most photogenic in the valley.
character: A picturesque, tranquil chalk-stream village in the South Downs, popular with those seeking a slower pace, with a thriving pub and well-stocked village stores.
amenities: The Bucks Head pub (shared with Corhampton)
Meonstoke Village Stores and Post Office (well-stocked, artisan produce)
CofE Infant School and The Valley Pre-school

schools.name: Meonstoke CofE Infant School (Meon Valley Federation)

prices.summary: A desirable South Downs chalk-stream village; period houses (many pre-1900) and detached homes, with common council tax bands F and G in the older parts. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walks along the River Meon and to Old Winchester Hill
South Downs National Park walking and cycling
Local vineyards (Exton fizz sold at the village stores)
placesToEat: The Baker's Arms gastropub at nearby Droxford
pros: Idyllic chalk-stream setting in the South Downs National Park
Thriving pub and excellent village stores
Infant school and pre-school in the village
Outstanding walking on the doorstep (Old Winchester Hill)
cons: Only an infant school locally; juniors/secondary are in neighbouring villages
No railway station; rely on Winchester or Petersfield
A32 through the valley carries traffic and motorcyclists
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a beautiful, peaceful Meon Valley village with a strong pub-and-shop community and superb walking.
primarySupermarket: Meonstoke Village Stores; supermarkets in Bishop's Waltham/Winchester

sources.label: Great British Life - Living in the Meon Valley
